title: AAR - Emergency Pet Foster & In-Home Care
description: Free or sliding-scale temporary foster care and in-home pet care for low-income individuals in crisis — including those fleeing domestic violence, entering recovery, facing incarceration, or experiencing homelessness. ⚠️ Not accepting new applications until September 1, 2026.
engage: Apply via the Temporary Foster Application or In-Home Care Application on arrayanimalrescue.org/contracts-forms. Email arrayanimalrescue@gmail.com with questions. Must have a case worker to qualify.
content:
⚠️ Applications Paused

Array Animal Rescue is not accepting new applications until September 1, 2026 due to caseload and funding limitations. Existing applicants remain on the waitlist. Check the website for current status before applying.

Services

  • Foster Care for People in Transition: Temporary foster placement for pets while owners enter recovery programs or transitional housing — up to 3 months.
  • Domestic Violence Emergency Foster: Emergency 24/7 pet pickup for people fleeing abusive situations — up to 3 months.
  • Crisis Foster Care: Temporary foster placement for pets during sudden hospitalizations or incarcerations, including home pickup — up to 3 months.
  • In-Home Pet Care: Daily visits for feeding, walking, and companionship when foster placement is unavailable. Dogs: twice daily up to 2 weeks. Cats: once daily up to 2 months.

Eligibility

  • Must reside in Oregon
  • Must be experiencing homelessness, eviction, domestic violence, sudden incarceration, sudden hospitalization, or displacement due to natural disaster
  • Must have an active case worker (OHP/SNAP alone does not qualify)
  • Free services for households earning 30% or less of area median income
  • Sliding scale fees available for those above income threshold ($40/day dogs, $20/day cats at full rate)
  • Should be a last resort after all other options are exhausted

How to Apply

Complete the Temporary Foster Application or In-Home Care Application at arrayanimalrescue.org/contracts-forms. Email arrayanimalrescue@gmail.com for questions or urgent situations.

Service Area

Oregon only. Primarily serving Multnomah County and the Portland Metro Area.
centerings: Domestic Violence (DV) Survivors; People in Recovery; Justice Impacted; People with Pets/Animals; Low Barrier

title: AAR - Volunteer & Community Service
description: Volunteer and court-ordered community service opportunities with Array Animal Rescue, including dog walking, animal transport, fostering, community outreach, and direct support to people experiencing homelessness. ⚠️ Not accepting new applications until September 1, 2026.
engage: Complete the Volunteer and Community Service application at arrayanimalrescue.org/contracts-forms. Email arrayanimalrescue@gmail.com with questions.
content:
⚠️ Applications Paused

Array Animal Rescue is not accepting new volunteer applications until September 1, 2026. Check the website for current status.

Opportunities

  • Dog Walking: Exercise rescue dogs and provide companionship.
  • Animal Transport: Drive animals to vet appointments, foster homes, or donation pickups. Valid driver's license and current insurance required.
  • Foster Care: Provide temporary homes for animals. Earn 4 hours of community service credit per day fostered.
  • Community Outreach: Distribute flyers, canvass neighborhoods, and raise awareness about services.
  • Direct Support: Offer peer support, transportation, and help distribute food and supplies to people experiencing homelessness.

Who Can Participate

  • General community volunteers
  • Individuals fulfilling court-ordered community service obligations

How to Sign Up

Complete the Volunteer and Community Service application at arrayanimalrescue.org/contracts-forms.

Service Area

Portland Metro Area, Oregon.
